channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The oldest millennials are 45! This tool helps plan for longevity
item.description: The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.
item.content:encoded:
The oldest millennials are turning 45 this year. The oldest Gen Xers are now 60. So how prepared will they be to thrive in the decades ahead? A new tool helps people assess beyond just savings.

(Image credit: Mar Hernández for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, WHO says
item.description: Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.
item.content:encoded:
Hantaviruses are usually spread by exposure to urine, saliva or feces from infected rodents, such as rats or mice. Hantavirus infections are rare but can cause deadly respiratory infections.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: California's rule to add folic acid brings a Hispanic staple into the regulatory fold
item.description: In January, California became the first U.S. state to require food makers to add folic acid to products like tortillas because of the high rate of neural tube defects in Hispanic infants.
item.content:encoded: In January, California became the first U.S. state to require food makers to add folic acid to products like tortillas because of the high rate of neural tube defects in Hispanic infants.
item.dc:creator: Lesley McClurg

item.title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
item.description: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?
item.content:encoded:
Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

(Image credit: Theo Wargo)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
item.description: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Court restricts abortion access across the US by blocking the mailing of mifepristone
item.description: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.
item.content:encoded:
A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The expert on 'super aging' breaks down the science — and grift — in anti-aging
item.description: From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.
item.content:encoded:
From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.

(Image credit: Capuski)
item.dc:creator: Manoush Zomorodi

item.title: Former Surgeon General Jerome Adams on Trump's new pick for the role
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Taking a look at looksmaxxing — and what parents should know
item.description: The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors.
item.content:encoded:
The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors. 

(Image credit: Malte Mueller)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
item.description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.
item.content:encoded:
Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Phil Galewitz
